reason:
Three explicit requests from the chairman: (1) Add log file path to Settings → About panel (one new line: '日志目录: ...' + a hint that logs rotate daily UTC as flowntier.log.YYYY-MM-DD). (2) Move '角色 → 模型 分配' (RoleAssignmentCard) from above the About card to BELOW the SearchBugPanel so the search UI sits next to the role config (both read-mostly). (3) Stop using system environment variables to hold API keys — use the OS Credential Manager (DPAPI via keyring) exclusively, even if that means removing the run_agent_task handler's env-var fallback path.

For Task 1: Settings.tsx About card now calls invoke('get_diagnostics') on mount and stores log_dir in state. The placeholder '%APPDATA%\flowntier\' (which was a UA-sniff hack at Settings.tsx:282–285) is replaced by the real Rust-reported data_dir. New i18n keys: settings.about.logDir ('日志目录' / 'Log directory') and settings.about.logDirHint.

For Task 2: One-line move — the h3 + RoleAssignmentCard map was at lines 480–495, now lives at lines 580–593 (after SearchBugPanel). No other code touches. Verified by grep: exactly one occurrence of settings.roles.title in Settings.tsx, and it sits below SearchBugPanel.

For Task 3 (security): /api/run_task handler at handlers.rs:997–1008 used to fall back to std::env::var(body.api_key_env) if body.api_key was empty. That entire arm is removed; the handler now REQUIRES body.api_key to be non-empty, with the explicit error 'missing or empty api_key (no env-var fallback in v0.4.12)'. ChatZone.tsx is updated to call revealSecret(keyEnvVar) and pass the plaintext as body.api_key. New regression test run_task_rejects_api_key_env_fallback pins the contract: setting an env var must NOT satisfy the auth gate. Test was added to crates/pipe-server/tests/e2e_pipe.rs and passes (9/9 e2e + 20/20 unit).

Version bumped 0.4.11 → 0.4.12 in tauri.conf.json + package.json. Cargo.toml already at 0.4.0 (workspace version, separate). All five crates rebuilt in release mode. NSIS installer (16,073,092 bytes) is on chairman's desktop at C:\Users\thatg\Desktop\Flowntier_0.4.12_x64-setup.exe.

Build pipeline note (so future me doesn't repeat): tauri build regenerates installer.nsi each run, which overwrites my sidecar CheckIfAppIsRunning patch. So the correct sequence is: (a) tauri build (generates fresh NSI), (b) node scripts/patch-nsis.cjs (patches NSI in place), (c) makensis installer.nsi directly (rebuilds installer.exe with patch embedded), (d) move nsis-output.exe to bundle/nsis/Flowntier_X_x64-setup.exe. The patch-nsis.cjs is idempotent (checks for marker comment before re-inserting).
